title: 241Am Neutron Capture Cross Section Measurement using the NaI(Tl) spectrometer of the ANNRI beamline of J-PARC

x4freetext: 241Am Neutron Capture Cross Section Measurement using
the NaI(Tl) spectrometer of the ANNRI beamline of
J-PARC

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.A, Vol.736, p.66 (2014)

x4freetext: Energy resolution of the pulsed neutron
Flight path length of 27.9 m.
Pulse-height weighting technique was applied.
-Size: 330 mm (diam.) and 203 mm in thickness
-Angle: 90 deg with respect to the neutron beam line
-Flight length: 27.9 m
-DAQ: CAEN V1720 Digitizer (12bit, 250MHz)
Spallation reaction in a mercury target
irradiated with 3-GeV protons (700 kW) .
The proton beams were supplied at a repetition rate
of 25 Hz and in the "double-bunch mode", in which
the pulsed protons consist of two bunches, each with
a width of 100 ns, at intervals of 600 ns.
Produced neutrons were slowed down in a supercritical
para-hydrogen moderator.
A Disk chopper with a semicircular shape was used to
cut the low-energy tail of the neutron pulse.
The neutron spectrum was reconstructed using the 478
keV gamma-rays from gamma rays from 10B(n,alpha
gamma) 7Li reaction.
7.47 +- 0.01 mg of 241Am oxide powder (957 MBq) mixed
with 46.0 mg of Y2O3 powder (binder) .
Sample powder was pressed into a pellet shape with a
diameter 10 mm, and sealed in an Al case (an area
density of 2.37 x 10E-5 atoms/b) .
A replica of the dummy case was used in order to
derive the sample-dependent background induced by
the case and the Y2O3.
A natural carbon sample was used as a neutron scatterer
to derive the background due to scattered neutrons.
A thick gold sample with a diameter of 10.0 +- 0.1 mm
and a thickness of 0.10 +- 0.01 mm was employed for
normalization with the saturated resonance method.
A 90.4%-enriched boron sample with a mass of 86.3 mg,
and a diameter of 10.0 +- 0.1 mm was used for neutron
spectrum measurement.
Pulse-height weighting technique (PHWT) was applied.
Measurements with the carbon sample,
"Dummy replica sample", "carbon sample" and "Blank (no
sample) " were used for back ground estimation.
The relative neutron spectrum was estimated using
the boron sample.
The weighting function was normalized using the
saturated resonance method at the 1st resonance
of the gold sample.
Dead Time Correction was applied with the non-
paralyzable model (deadtime: 1.25 +-0.01 us) .
Correction factors for self-shielding and multiple
scattering were deduced using the Monte-Carlo
simulation code PHITS.
Influence of the fission events were deduced and
subtracted using the 241Am capture and fission
cross sections and the gamma-ray multiplicities
for the capture and fission reactions.

x4headers.headerHelp: Energy of incident projectile, laboratory system
Value of quantity specified under REACTION

x4freetext: Resonance energies, neutron and radiation widths
were determined with REFIT using Reich-Moor
approximation.
- The ANNRI beamline resolution function was taken
from the work of Kino. See REL-REF.
- The initial resonance parametrization data
were taken from JENDL-5.
- The initial delay and flight path were derived using
the results from the Au sample measurement.
- All resonances have been considered to be s-wave.
- Doppler effect was modeled using the
free gas model with a temperature of 27 deg-C.
- Impurities were estimated from the fitting process
in the form of 1.52 +- 0.03 % of 237Np and
- Simultaneous fitting results for the radiative width,
neutron width were performed for 23 large resonances
below 18 eV.
The averaged radiation width was calculated as
44.7 +- 1.1 meV. This averaged radiation
width was used and kept fixed for the other resonances.
- In some resonances, the resonance energy was also
fixed due to resonance overlapping.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainty of resonance energy was
deduced from the uncertainty of the fitting result by
REFIT, the flight length, and the initial time delay.
The other uncertainties are the ones provided by REFIT.

x4freetext: Resonance analysis with REFIT.
Simultaneous fitting results for the radiative width,
neutron width were performed for 23 large resonances
below 18 eV.
All resonances have been considered to be s-wave.
The averaged radiation width was calculated.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainty of the DATA was deduced
from the uncertainties of the 23 radiative widths.
